# Validation Approach {.appendix}

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Validation methodology: what we can measure and what requires expert review."
flowchart TD
    A["PDIP expert annotations"] --> B["Holdout split"]
    B --> C["Calibration set (5 docs)\nUsed to tune patterns"]
    B --> D["Evaluation set (remaining)\nHeld back for measurement"]
    D --> E["Recall: did we find\nwhat experts tagged?"]
    E --> F["Verbatim check: does extracted\ntext match source (95%)?"]
    F --> G["What we still need:\nPrecision (false positive rate)"]
    G --> H["Lawyer review"]
```

| What we measure | What we do not measure |
|----------------|----------------------|
| Recall against PDIP annotations | Precision (false positive rate) |
| Verbatim similarity to source text | Inter-rater reliability |
| Extraction completeness | Temporal consistency across eras |

**Honest limitations.** The holdout set is small. PDIP annotations come from a single expert source. The evaluation set skews toward countries and issuance styles that PDIP covers.

**This is why lawyer evaluation matters.** Our validation can measure recall but not precision. Only expert review can tell us how many of the 9,145 extracted matches are actually correct.
